SnowDistribution: Firm snow - ice on some aspects.  Cloud base was at 800 metres all day.  Winds 20 -30 mph progress difficult at times with stronger gusts
Comment: Better stability in most locations during daylight hours. Cornices expected to weaken overnight.
WeatherInfluences: Mild and wet at all levels overnight, then rapidly cooler in the early hours with light precipitation falling as snow down to 950m. Dry and remaining cool from dawn onwards.
SnowStability: Some minor wet snow instability overnight on some very steep North West to North East aspects above 1000m. Better stability during daylight hours. The old snowpack will be moist at lower altitudes, firmer near summits. New humid windslab possible on North to North East aspects above 950m but expected to be thin, localised. Cornices will soften and weaken overnight. Further snow loss at lower elevations by the end of the forecast period.  The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Overnight snow showers turning to sleet and rain after midday. Strong South South East winds. Cold overnight then progressively milder from late morning onwards.
ObservedSnowStability: Localised accumulations of thin windslab on some very steep North West through North to North East aspects above 950m. Windslab was humid and well bonded at the time of reporting but was before the onset of milder temperatures and rain. Expanses of hard snow ice down to 800m on these aspects. Good stability in all other locations.  The avalanche hazard is Low

SnowDistribution: Firm old snow with fresh wet snow or windslab on top. Poor visibility above 900 metres. Winds around 40 mph, making progress difficult at times.
WeatherInfluences: In the early part of the night there will be storm force Southerly winds and heavy rain with the freezing level above the summits. The freezing level will then drop to around 1000 metres and there will be occasional light snow showers and strong South-South-Westerly winds.
SnowStability: There will be a period of wet snow instability in the early part of the night and cornices will be prone to collapse. West through North to East aspects above 1000 metres will be most affected. After this the snowpack will consolidate in the cooler temperatures and stability will improve. Fresh windslab accumulations during the day are not expected to be significant. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Snow in the morning gradually turned more to rain with a drier afternoon following. Gale force Southerly winds. The freezing level rose quickly to above all summits.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of poorly bonded windslab are present mainly on West through North to East aspects above 900 metres. Wet snow instabilities were developing in these areas in the afternoon. Fresh, fragile cornices are present above these aspects. The older snowpack remains firm and stable. Cornice triggered avalanches recorded on a North-West aspect at 1300 metres this morning. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Winds approx 40 - 50 mph on the tops.  Limited patchy snow cover.   Cloud base was around 700 metres all day.
Comment: What a contrast from yesterday, a grey day with rain and cloud cover. Improving tomorrow with colder conditions and some bright spells and a mostly dry day.
WeatherInfluences: Freezing level will be well above the summits overnight lowering to around 800m in the early hours and remaining so throughout the day. Precipitation falling as rain to all levels overnight, then mostly a dry day is expected with the odd sleet or snow shower above 800m. Winds will be strong to gale force South-Westerly.
SnowStability: The patchy and limited snowpack that remains mostly above 800m on North-West through North to Easterly aspects will refreeze, becoming very firm and stable throughout.  The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Cold with a dusting of new snow at summit levels at first, disappearing as the freezing level rose above summit levels by midday. Winds strong South-Easterly. Precipitation falling as rain to all levels.
ObservedSnowStability: After a dusting of snow overnight at summit levels this disappeared during the day. The old snowpack that now remains is well bonded and stable throughout. Although moist from today\'s rain, it remains firm.  Snow cover is mostly patchy but significant areas of hard snow is  present in some gullies, corries and summit slopes, mostly above 750 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low

SnowDistribution: Firm snow on many aspects, although softening slightly later. Whiteout conditions aloft. Windy, speeds of approx 60-70 mph at times, moderating later.
Comment: A period of instability overnight as an active front crosses the area bringing intense rain and positive temperatures to even the highest summits. Gradually stabilising later, but with the lingering threat of cornice collapse.
WeatherInfluences: Intense precipitation is expected through the night, with a dry to follow. This will fall as rain at all levels, gradually turning to sleet then snow above 950 metres by mid morning. Winds will be storm force Southerlies overnight, veering South-South-Westerly and becoming very strong during the day. The freezing level will be around 2000 metres overnight, dropping to around 1000 metres by morning.
SnowStability: A period of wet snow instability is expected overnight and into the morning due to rain. During this period there is the potential for \'Wet Snow Slab\' and \'Wet-loose Snow\' avalanches on West through North-West and North to East aspects above 950 metres. Remaining cornices will also be prone to collapse. Later, the snowpack will gradually consolidate, becoming stable as the freezing level lowers to around 950 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Gale force Southerly winds in the morning, dropping to strong in the afternoon. Snow fell above 900 metres, transitioning to sleet followed by rain to end the period. The freezing level was around 1000 metres at midday, rising above the summits later.
ObservedSnowStability: Unstable windslab became more widespread as the day progressed on West through North to North-East aspects above 950 metres. The greatest accumulations are found in steep wind sheltered locations such as coire rims and gully tops. Outwith these areas, more isolated accumulations are present on crag aprons of the same aspect. New fragile cornices were observed around coire rims. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Diminishing snowpack, good above 800 metres. Cloud base was at around 850 metres for much of the day. Winds 30 -40 mph, progress difficult and tiring.
WeatherInfluences: Rain to all levels through Sunday night, then drier in the daytime when the freezing level will drop to around summit level. Gale force Southerly winds overnight will veer South-South West while decreasing to strong.
SnowStability: The snowpack will become increasingly wet overnight and will be thawing at all levels, however it will be generally stable throughout. Limited potential for wet snow surface instabilities on steep North-West to North-East aspects above 950 metres. Although the snowpack will diminish, good coverage will remain on South-West through North-West to North-East aspects above 850 metres and on the White Mounth. Remaining cornices will be prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Light precipitation, which fell as snow to around 700 metres overnight, progressively turned to rain at all levels as the freezing level rose to well above the summits. Very strong Southerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh isolated and shallow deposits of windslab were forming through Saturday night and into the morning in steep wind sheltered areas on North-West to North-East aspects above 950 metres. As it turned mild later these deposits were consolidating and thawing. Elsewhere the snowpack is stable. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Some new moist snow fell above 600 metres. Most continuous older snow cover is above 800 metres which is still firm and icy but thawing at all elevations. Poor visibility with moderate to strong  Southerly winds 20-35 mph.
Comment: After a very wet mild night more favorable conditions are expected on Monday.
WeatherInfluences: Sunday night, strong to gale force Southerly winds and rain at all levels will affect the area. The freezing level will lower to around 900 metres and rain will turn to isolated light snow showers by dawn. Drier conditions and easing South-South-Westerly winds are expected for the rest of the day.
SnowStability: Overnight Sunday, wet snow instabilities are expected above 850 metres. Most affected locations will be very steep slopes, gully exits and steep corrie rims with a North-West through North to Easterly aspect. The older firm snowpack will continue to thaw and soften, any remaining cornices will be prone to collapse. During the day the snowpack will start to consolidate and refreeze above 800 metres as drier conditions take affect. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Overnight, the freezing level was around 700 metres, snow showers fell mainly above 650 metres on strengthening Southerly winds. At first these conditions continued to affect the area but during the course of the morning the freezing level rose to above the summits by midday. The snow showers turned to rain at all levels and the winds became much stronger in the afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: New snow fell with the greatest accumulations being above 900 metres these were generally not extensive and started to quickly thaw after midday. Some surface wet snow instabilities developed on steeper slopes. Gully exits and steep corrie rims with a North-West through North to Easterly aspects were most affected. The older icy snowpack is slowly thawing but still firm and generally stable. Remaining cornices are unstable. The avalanche hazard is Low.
